dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 3484 RH: Business Owners Protection Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Barr (for himself, Mr. Emmer, Mr. Westerman, and Mrs. Hinson) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Financial Services
Additional sponsors: Mr. Hudson, Mr. Baumgartner, Mr. Williams of Texas, and Mr. Sessions
Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on May 19, 2025

form.official-title: To terminate unused authorities of the Securities and Exchange Commission that were established pursuant to the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Business Owners Protection Act of 2025.

section.header: Repeal of certain unused authority to restrict mandatory predispute arbitration

subsection.text: Section 15 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (15 U.S.C. 78o) is amended by repealing subsection (o).
Section 921 of the Investor Protection and Securities Reform Act of 2010 is amended by striking subsection (a).

section.header: Removal of certain authority related to fiduciary duties

subsection.text: The second subsection (l) of section 15 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (15 U.S.C. 78o; relating to Other matters) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking Commission shall and all that follows through (1) facilitate and inserting Commission shall facilitate;
in paragraph (1), by striking ; and and inserting a period; and

subsection.text: Section 211(h) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 (15 U.S.C. 80b–11(h)) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking Commission shall and all that follows through (1) facilitate and inserting Commission shall facilitate;
in paragraph (1), by striking ; and and inserting a period; and

section.header: Repeal of certain unused authority related to standards of conduct
section.text: Section 15 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (15 U.S.C. 78o) is amended by repealing the second subsection (k) (relating to Standard of Conduct).

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with an amendment, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
